chapter_title.en: The Supplication of Qunoot in Witr Prayer

parts.text: أَبِي وَلاَّدٍ حَفْصِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ اَلْحَنَّاطِ أَنَّهُ

text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ: «لاَ بَأْسَ بِأَنْ يُصَلِّيَ اَلرَّجُلُ رَكْعَتَيْنِ مِنَ اَلْوَتْرِ ثُمَّ يَنْصَرِفَ فَيَقْضِيَ حَاجَتَهُ ثُمَّ يَرْجِعَ فَيُصَلِّيَ رَكْعَةً.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1417 - It is narrated from Abu Walad Hafs bin Salim Al-Hannat that He said:
"I heard Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) say,
'There is no harm if a person prays two units (rak‘ahs) of Witr prayer, then turns away to fulfil a need, and then returns to pray one unit (rak‘ah).'".
